title: [policy] Key storage compliance
description: Policy: spec/06-security.md §15.2. MUST: Private keys in secure storage (hardware secure element, encrypted flash, or OS keychain). MUST NOT: hardcode keys, log keys, transmit plaintext keys. When executing: grep for key storage/loading in all implementations, create compliance bead per code section.
close_reason: Verified compliant: All implementations properly handle private keys - Rust uses Zeroize/ZeroizeOnDrop with REDACTED debug output; C uses crypto_wipe/secure_wipe after key operations; Python documents memory limitation and recommends HSM. No hardcoded keys, no secret logging, no plaintext transmission of private material. Storage abstracted via NonVolatile trait for platform-specific secure storage.
